target_hypotheses.32: Warm — 2x liked our posts. Coinbase x402 team. Ask about protocol-level vs app-level auth.
target_hypotheses.35: Warm — followed after distribution day. Unknown builder. Discovery question.
target_hypotheses.1: Live x402/search endpoint. No identity check. Ask how they think about authorization.
target_hypotheses.4: Live x402 paid inference demo. DevRel who ships tutorials. Ask about agent verification.
target_hypotheses.5: Built @pay() decorator. Zero auth. Ask about identity checks before payment.

drafts.32: Hey Kevin, your x402 explanations have been the clearest I've seen. In the current flow, any wallet-equipped agent can call a paid endpoint once it can pay. Do you see authorization-before-payment as protocol-level, or something apps should own?
drafts.35: Hey, thanks for the follow. I saw you around the Base/x402 distribution day and noticed you've been tracking x402. Are you building with paid endpoints, or mostly exploring the space?
drafts.1: Hey Caleb. Firecrawl's live x402/search endpoint is one of the clearest real examples of paid agent access I've seen. How are you thinking about authorization for agents calling it, beyond wallet payment?
drafts.4: Hey Harish. Your Gaia node paid x402 API demo is clean. Are you thinking about access control before inference is served, or treating wallet payment as enough authorization for now?
drafts.5: Hey, the @pay() decorator is a sharp idea: one-line monetization for FastAPI endpoints. Are you thinking about identity or authorization checks before payment is accepted?
drafts.13: Hey Questflow team. Processing 48K+ x402 transactions is one of the strongest live signals I've seen in the ecosystem. Are you seeing demand for identifying or authorizing agents beyond the paying wallet?
drafts.25: Hey Peter, following up from a couple weeks ago. With ampersend + OpenClaw agents now able to pay via x402, are you handling tool authorization separately from payment?
drafts.40: Hey Thariq, following up from June 13. For MCP tools in production, are you looking at signed or verifiable caller identity before tool access?

codex_review: Two-pass Codex review. Pass 1: strip 'I built' from first contact. Pass 2: strip playground link from ALL first-contact DMs. No link until they reply. Pattern: specific praise + one technical question = the whole DM.
